title: GPT explained visually..
summary: A visual educational overview of how GPT models work, covering self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections. The video explains the fundamental mechanics of generative pretrained transformers that power models like Gemini, Opus, and Qwen.
writeup: This video gives a visual walkthrough of how GPT models work, breaking down the core components of generative pretrained transformers. It covers self-attention and multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections, the same mechanics behind models like Gemini, Opus, and Qwen. The aim is to make the fundamental architecture legible by showing how these pieces fit together rather than treating the model as a black box.
